exemplar.title:
How effective were German government interventions from 2019 to 2023 in
substituting non-renewable electricity with renewable electricity to reduce the
negative externalities of production in the German electricity market?

detailedExemplarData.title:
How effective were German government interventions from 2019 to 2023 in
substituting non-renewable electricity with renewable electricity to reduce the
negative externalities of production in the German electricity market?

subject.title: Economics
subject.description:
**IB Economics** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **microeconomics, macroeconomics, the global economy, and development economics**, with a strong focus on **analytical and evaluative skills, real-world application of economic theory, and quantitative analysis**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Extended response questions | 1h 15min | 1h 30min | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Data response questions | 1h 45min | 1h 45min | 40% (SL), 30% (HL) |
| **Paper 3** | Mathematical questions | N/A | 1h | 20% (HL only) |
| **Internal Assessment** | Portfolio of 3 commentaries | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Topic and purpose communicated accurately and effectively, with a clear economic focus on welfare analysis and externalities
Research question is clearly stated, tightly focused on instruments, timeframe and outcomes, and consistently guides the discussion
Methodology demonstrates informed selection of a wide range of relevant secondary and primary sources, with justification of source limitations
weaknesses: Opening pages contain minor verbosity and some repetition, slightly reducing precision
No advanced quantitative (econometric) techniques are applied to strengthen the methodology
Mitigation steps for interview biases are noted but not fully explained
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands in Focus and Method were assessed in the upper band (5 marks each) for clear communication of topic, a tightly focused research question, and an appropriate range of methods. While minor verbosity and lack of rigorous quantitative methods prevent a perfect 6/6 in the examinerâs original assessment, the overall precision, coherence and alignment with systematic economic investigation justify awarding the top mark of 6.

strengths: Source material is clearly relevant and generally integrated effectively through diagrams, tables and narrative
Demonstrates clear and coherent knowledge of micro- and macroeconomic theory, applying concepts of externalities, Pigouvian taxes and elasticity to the German case
Terminology (e.g., âmarginal social costâ, âfeed-in tariffâ, âcap-and-tradeâ) is accurate and consistently used throughout text and diagrams
weaknesses: Some over-reliance on secondary compilations limits deeper critical interrogation of raw data
Minor inconsistencies in data presentation (e.g., 54 % vs. 56 %) and occasional missing units/currency symbols
A few theoretical oversimplifications (e.g., socially optimal quantity described as zero) suggest room for greater nuance
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two of the three strands were judged at the top of the 3-4 band (scores of 5) and one at the upper end of that band (4), indicating mostly relevant and effective use of sources, clear discipline knowledge, and accurate terminology. The occasional data inconsistencies and descriptive use of figures justify placing this work solidly in the 5/6 band for Knowledge and Understanding.

strengths: Research sources are consistently appropriate and relevant, spanning quantitative data, policy documents and a primary interview
Analysis is clear and well supported by diagrams, numerical trends and economic welfare reasoning
Argument is coherent and acknowledges key limitations, policy trade-offs and data caveats in a reasoned conclusion
weaknesses: No statistical testing or econometric methods are applied to validate correlations and causal claims
Evaluation remains partial; deeper critical appraisal (e.g., alternative explanations, sensitivity checks) would strengthen the discussion
Some causal attributions assume ceteris paribus without fully investigating confounding factors
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates a high level of critical thinking: research quality aligns with the 7-9 band, analysis is clear and supported, and the discussion offers reasoned arguments with partially critical evaluation. Although it does not reach the exemplary 10-12 band (which requires fully rigorous testing and consistently critical evaluation), the depth of triangulation and the strength of the argument warrant the upper end of the 7-9 band, meriting 9/12.

strengths: Follows all Extended Essay conventions (title page, abstract-style opening, clear headings, table of contents, page numbers, bibliography)
Layout and formatting are largely consistent, with proper figure and table captions and an included word count
Neat integration of visual elements and citations enhances readability
weaknesses: Inconsistent heading styles and occasional long blocks of text slightly reduce navigability
Some figures lack full axis labels or units, and bibliography ordering and citation style vary
Interview transcript formatting could be clearer with speaker labels and spacing
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation meets the criteria for the top band (3-4 marks): structure is clearly appropriate and all layout conventions are applied correctly. Minor inconsistencies in heading hierarchy and figure labeling prevent a flawless performance, so a maximum of 4/4 is awarded.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research question is explicitly stated twice, specifies time frame, policy instruments and intended outcome, and is tightly linked to the subsequent analysis.  Its wording (âHow effectiveâ¦ in substitutingâ¦ to reduce negative externalitiesâ¦â) demonstrates focus on both quantitative and qualitative dimensions and guides the essay convincingly.  Some phrasing (âsubstitutingâ rather than âsubstitution ofâ) could be tighter and a dual-objective RQ can create breadth, yet the connection to the discussion remains strong; hence a high but not perfect score is justified.

The student frames the investigation around a clearly bounded policy episode (German electricity interventions 2019-23) and communicates the context, purpose and economic focus succinctly in the title, abstract-style opening, and justification section.  The topic is unquestionably suitable for an economics EE and is presented with consistent reference to welfare analysis and externalities, demonstrating an effective, not merely adequate, alignment between topic choice and the need for systematic investigation.  Minor verbosity and repetition in the opening pages prevent the work from exemplifying the very highest level of precision, but overall clarity and appropriateness merit placement in the upper band.

Source material (tables, figures, policy documents, interview) is generally relevant and is integrated through diagrams, statistics and narrative.  Citations accompany most claims and raw data are tabulated in an appendix.  Nevertheless, some figures are used descriptively (e.g., linear trend assertions without statistical testing) and dependence on secondary compilations limits critical interrogation of data quality.  This aligns with the descriptor of being âmostly relevant and appropriateâ, hence top of the 3-4 band.

The essay follows EE conventions: title page, abstract-style context, clear headings, table of contents, introduction-investigation-conclusion flow and bibliography.  All mandatory elements are present, though occasional section overlap and some long blocks of text reduce optimal coherence, meriting 3 rather than 4.

Formatting is largely consistent: word count, title, page numbers, tables and figure captions are provided.  Citations follow a recognisable style and images are integrated neatly.  Minor issues (occasional line-break artefacts, inconsistent italicisation) are present but overall adherence to layout expectations is clear, supporting 3 marks.

The argument is coherent and sections culminate in interim judgements, culminating in a conclusion that revisits the RQ and recognises budgetary trade-offs and data limitations.  Evaluation considers reliability of sources, weather dependency and fiscal costs, showing partial critical engagement.  Yet evaluation remains brief relative to analysis and sometimes asserts effectiveness without rigorous testing, fitting the mid-band descriptor of âreasoned argument with partially critical evaluationâ (5 marks).

Analysis is clear and usually supported by diagrams, numerical trends and welfare discussion.  The student links policy instruments to shifts in supply curves and welfare areas, and quantifies changes in production and emissions.  However, causal reasoning sometimes hinges on simple linear trends and assumes ceteris paribus without testing alternative explanations; econometric depth is absent.  These features align with âclear analysis with mostly supported conclusionsâ, justifying the top of the 4-6 band.

The student demonstrates clear and coherent knowledge of microeconomic theory (externalities, Pigouvian taxes, subsidies, X-elasticities) and links it to the German case.  Macroeconomic extensions (LRAS, AD) are correctly applied.  Occasional oversimplificationsâsuch as stating the socially optimal quantity of NRE is âtechnically zeroââprevent full sophistication, but the use of theory remains effective and well-structured, supporting a score of 5.

The majority of research is appropriate and clearly relevant; data coverage spans 2019-23, policy descriptions are current, and the interview adds contextual depth.  The investigation triangulates production data, emissions trends and policy timelines, indicating a coherent evidence base.  Nonetheless, limited triangulation of price data and reliance on correlation for effectiveness stop it from reaching the consistently exemplary 10-12 band, so the lower boundary of 7-9 is awarded (7 marks).

A broad range of up-to-date secondary sources (Statistisches Bundesamt, IEA, Clean Energy Wire, etc.) and one piece of primary data (Sunfire interview) are selected purposefully.  The student explains source limitations (political bias, language barriers) and justifies reliance on raw data over commentaries, showing informed selection.  The methodology section outlines how externality diagrams, trend analysis and elasticity concepts will be applied.  However, econometric or more rigorous quantitative techniques are absent and price-data constraints are only partially mitigated, warranting 5 rather than 6.

Terminology such as âmarginal social costâ, âfeed-in tariffâ, âcross-price elasticity of supplyâ and âcap-and-tradeâ is employed accurately and consistently throughout diagrams and text.  Graphs are labelled correctly and units are mostly precise.  Minor lapses (mixing million/billion kWh, occasional typographical errors) exist but do not impede conceptual clarity, warranting 5.

annotations.comment: Thereâs a data inconsistency: the table shows RE at 54 % but later references 56 % in the narrative. Verify source figures and ensure consistency across text and tables.
The context section includes compelling statistics but lacks any statistical testing or confidence intervals. Consider applying simple regression or significance tests to strengthen claims.
The title effectively communicates the investigationâs scope and aligns well with the research purpose, framing a clear focus on German policy interventions and welfare analysis.
The wordâcount declaration meets the formatting requirement and confirms compliance with the 4 000-word limit.
The heading style âSupply(NRE) + Supply(RE) =507 million kWhâ diverges from earlier heading conventions. Standardize hierarchy and numbering of headings for coherence.
When discussing cross-price elasticity, include the formula $$E_{xy} = \frac{\%\Delta Q_x}{\%\Delta P_y}$$ and sample calculation to demonstrate quantitative understanding.
Figure 7âs axes lack labels and units (euros per tonne, years). Add clear axis titles and gridlines to enhance interpretability.
The welfare-analysis paragraph quantifies quantity shifts but omits numerical welfare-loss estimates. Incorporate area calculations for improved depth.
The revenue figure â640 billionâ lacks a currency symbol (â¬). Always specify units and currency to avoid ambiguity.
Compare the scale of ETS revenues versus subsidy outlays using a ratio or percentage to illustrate net fiscal impact.
The COâ-emissions section describes trends but omits absolute tonnage comparisons. Include raw emissions alongside avoided figures for clarity.
The conclusion demonstrates critical evaluation of limitations and policy cost effectiveness, strengthening the overall argument.
You note diagrams are ânot to scaleâ and mention price-data limitations. To fortify analysis, propose alternative non-price proxies or sensitivity checks to mitigate this gap.
Stating the socially optimal quantity of NRE as zero is an oversimplification. Discuss feasibility and transitional constraints instead of assuming full substitution.
The write-up of the Sunfire interview acknowledges bias but does not explain how you mitigated it. Outline steps taken to validate or triangulate these insights.
Table 2 covers only four years, leading to potential noise from weather-related volatility. Apply a moving average or trend line regression to test robustness.
Attributing the 2021 drop solely to weather ignores other factors (policy phasing, grid constraints). Discuss alternative explanations and data caveats.
Proposing further statistical forecasting demonstrates strong reflective engagement and suggests clear next steps for research.
The bibliography list order is not strictly alphabetical; reordering sources by author surname enhances navigability and presentation consistency.
Citing ChatGPT as a "source" is inappropriate; replace with peer-reviewed literature or official publications.
The interview transcript lacks clear distinction between questions and responses; format with speaker labels and line breaks for readability.
Sunfire interview responses are qualitative; consider corroborating with quantitative performance data or external market reports.
The methodology justification outlines source selection but remains largely descriptive. Strengthen by linking each source choice to specific analytical steps.
Several online sources lack access dates; include retrieval dates to meet citation formatting standards.
Citation style fluctuates (some include DOIs, others omit them). Adopt a consistent style (APA or MLA) and apply across all entries.
The appendix is correctly included but should be referred to explicitly in the methodology. Clarify which analyses draw on these raw data.
You note political and language biases in reports but do not explain mitigation. Describe how you cross-checked translated material or triangulated findings.
The research question is clearly restated twice, but the second repetition is unnecessaryâavoid duplication for conciseness.
